content: The number of additions and multiplications involved in performing Gaussian elimination on any $n \times n$ upper triangular matrix is of the order
explanation:
As we know the matrix is upper triangular so we can start solving from result in previous row and so on.

The number of (Addition/Multiplication) is

$$ \begin{aligned} & 1+2+3+\ldots . .+(n-1) \\ & \frac{n(n-1)}{2} \end{aligned} $$

So time complexing is $O\left(n^2\right)$.

Hence, the correct option is (B).
